name: Pete Yellin
abstract: Peter Michael Yellin (July 18, 1941 – April 13, 2016) was an American jazz saxophonist and educator.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Harry James and His Orchestra, Buddy Rich and His Band, and the Ruby Braff-George Barnes Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first musicians I met when I got to New York who was my age, my friend Pete Yellin... We used to hang out... That was Pete Yellin... The idea was me and Pete wanted to take all of the Charlie Parker tunes that we loved
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Pete Yellin, Jay Cory, Marty Flax, Steve Perlow were the saxophone section you know? And all five of those guys were using hard drugs plus myself...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: I think Pete Yellin was doing the gig maybe at the time. I’m not sure if Pete was doing it.

about_quotes.speaker: Glenn Zottola
about_quotes.evidence: it was me, Pete Yellin... A couple of beboppers were doing all those gigs at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Glenn Zottola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: We’re all in the studio together. I think the whole thing is Ascension.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
